'''''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14''' is the fourteenth issue in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] series of comics.

==Publisher's summary==
''Unexpected twists at every turn as power-mad evildoers hunt our heroes amidst a galaxy spanning war—''Knights of the Old Republic'' is ''Star Wars'' at its very best, nearly 4,000 years before Luke Skywalker! A deadly new threat targets Camper, the seriously senile captain of the renegade vessel the ''Last Resort'', and his youthful guardian Jarael must risk everything to try and save him, even if it's impossible. Meanwhile, fugitive Padawan Zayne Carrick and the conniving Gryph have mistakenly tricked their way into their most dangerous predicament yet—surrounded by an army they are trying to escape on a planet that's about to become the biggest battlefront in the galaxy. The second someone recognizes them, they're toast, assuming that a stray blaster bolt doesn't get them first!''

*''Continuing the ongoing monthly series set nearly four thousand years before the rise of the Empire and the fall of Anakin Skywalker (and seven years prior to the events in the ''Knights of the Old Republic'' video game).''

==Plot summary==
Aboard [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']], the [[HK-24 series assassin droid|HK-24 unit]] reveals that his intent is to capture [[Gorman Vandrayk|Camper]] unharmed and kill [[Jarael]]. Engaging both her and [[T1-LB|Elbee]] in a fight, the assassin droid gets the upper-hand but is shot and destroyed by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Rohlan Dyre|Rohlan]], who has been hiding on the ship in secret since [[Flashpoint]].

Meanwhile, at a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Republic]] camp on the warfront planet of [[Serroco]], Lieutenant [[Carth Onasi]] stops by the ''[[Little Bivoli]]'', where [[Zayne Carrick|Zayne]], [[Marn Hierogryph|Gryph]], and [[Slyssk]] have set up a restaurant for the troops. In the kitchen, Zayne expresses his concerns to Gryph about their current predicament. Being Republic fugitives, Zayne is worried that they will eventually be recognized; Gryph however is too preoccupied with the money that their restaurant is making to worry about getting caught. Later, while cleaning up the restaurant, Zayne engages in a conversation with Onasi in which Onasi reveals that the Mandalorians are planning to attack on the following day.

That evening, Zayne once again reveals his desire to leave Serroco to Gryph. However, he is unable to convince the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] to leave, and decides to go for a walk through the camp. During his walk, Zayne pauses next to a ship and proceeds to have a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the next day's Mandalorian attack in which they destroy the entire planet. Hurrying back to the ''Little Bivoli'', Zayne wakes Gryph and tells him to pack up and leave immediately. Hesitant at first, Gryph reluctantly agrees to leave the planet, but when he starts giving packing directions to Zayne, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] reveals that he is not leaving with him and Slyssk, but is going to see the Republic commander, [[Saul Karath|Admiral Karath]].
